name: Droplist
summary: Composition manifest for the droplist family — the abstract substrate every list-based selection surface conforms to (Dropdown, Multi-dropdown, Autocomplete, Tree-select, native <select>). Composes existing intents rather than re-specifying their behavior, and documents the two-channel resolution rule (ambient intent vs. trait selection) that concrete members resolve through.
designDecisions: Composition over monolith: a droplist's hard parts (positioning, focus, selection, async, truncation) are general paradigms it composes, not droplist-specific behavior. Droplist therefore declares a composesIntents manifest and defers each behavior to the owning intent — keeping the family spec thin and the paradigms reusable by menus, comboboxes, trees, grids, and date pickers.

Family vs. members: droplist is the abstract family, not a shippable widget. Concrete members (Dropdown single-select, Multi-dropdown, Autocomplete, Tree-select, native baseline) are each their own block — distinct trait selections over the same substrate, not configurations of one widget. A menu button is NOT a droplist member: it opens a role=menu of commands (a value-invoker, not a value-selector) owned by the Menu block (#300); only its trigger shape (button[aria-haspopup]) is shared, demoted to trigger-only per #173 Fork G / #301.

Two channels, never crossed: structural dimensions (model, editable, which traits compose) are always local Trait Selection and cannot travel on the Ambient Intent (DI) channel; behavioral dimensions (focusStrategy, collision, truncate) may fall back to ambient intent. Per-dimension resolution: explicit ⊕ ambient (behavioral only) ⊕ default.

implementsIntent stays 'anchor' (the surface it most directly realizes) so existing intent→block reverse-lookup is unchanged; composesIntents is an additive field listing the full set, surfaced on both the block page and each composed intent's page.

ariaListbox.usage: The abstract substrate every list-selection member conforms to: a `role="listbox"` of `role="option"` items with single/multi selection and managed focus, realized concretely by Dropdown / Multi-dropdown / Autocomplete / Tree-select.
